senses.definition: to make something red or red-looking; to downplay or embellish with a reddish color
examples: The artist rubbed the surface to rubrify the iron with a red patina.
They rubbed a glaze to rubrify the sculpture's bronze sheen.

senses.definition: a device or substance that causes redness or a reddening effect; something that arouses embarrassment or shame
examples: The rubrisher in the dye kit ensures a bright red color on fabric.
His cheeks turned rubrisher with the memory.

senses.definition: relating to the rubrospinal tract, part of the nervous system involved in motor control
examples: Rubrospinal pathways influence voluntary movement.
Researchers studied rubrospinal connections in the motor cortex.

senses.definition: to apply pressure and move something back and forth to clean, soothe, or polish
examples: She rubs the lotion into her skin.
He rubs the wood with oil to bring out the grain.

senses.definition: a stone used for grinding and smoothing by rubbing
examples: The jewelry maker sharpened the blade on a rubstone.
They laid a rubstone to polish the edge.

senses.definition: plural of rubstone; multiple stones used for grinding and smoothing by rubbing
examples: The workshop had several rubstones for different grits.
They placed the rubstones on a rack.

senses.definition: a genus of plants in the rose family that includes many berries such as blackberries and raspberries
examples: The garden has a wild rubus with thorny canes.
Wild rubus fruits are edible when ripe.

senses.definition: a deep red precious gemstone; a red color
examples: She wore a ruby ring.
The ruby shade of the sunset was stunning.

senses.definition: bright red in color
examples: She chose a ruby dress for the party.
The ruby lipstick looked vibrant.

senses.definition: present participle of rubia; to color or stain reddish
examples: They were rubying the fabric with a natural dye.
The wood is rubying to a darker hue.

senses.definition: resembling or similar to ruby in color or appearance
examples: The gem has a rubylike shine.
His rubylike lips drew attention.

senses.definition: a species name used for certain birds with a red tail; also a common name for some small beasts with red tails
examples: The rubytail bird flashed its bright tail.
A rubytail insect is easy to spot in the reeds.

senses.definition: a hummingbird with a ruby-red throat; a small bird often seen in gardens
examples: The rubythroat visited the feeders at dawn.
Observers noted the rubythroat's shimmering throat patch.

senses.definition: plural of rubythroat; multiple rubythroat birds
examples: The rubythroats contested the feeder area.
Several rubythroats flashed their throats.

senses.definition: in the manner of ruby; with ruby-like characteristics
examples: The jewel was cut rubywise to maximize light.
She arranged the stones rubywise across the tray.

senses.definition: abbreviation or code with various possible meanings; context needed
examples: The acronym ruc appeared in the technical document.

senses.definition: a biological term referring to deer indicators; part of a taxonomic name group
examples: Rucervine species include several subtleties in horn structure.

senses.definition: a genus name in zoological classification, often used in scientific contexts
examples: The specimen was identified as Rucervus duvaucelii.

senses.definition: a star name; one of the names used in traditional Arabic astronomy
examples: The move was marked by a climb toward ruchbah in the night sky.

senses.definition: a pleated, ruffled fabric or a decorative strip on clothing
examples: A ruche trim decorated the dress hem.
The curtains were edged with soft ruches.

senses.definition: decorated with gathered fabric creating folds or ridges
examples: She wore a ruched blouse.
The ruched bedspread added texture.

senses.definition: plural of ruche; decorative fabric folds or ruffles
examples: The dress had multiple ruches along the sleeve.
They added ruches for a romantic look.

senses.definition: the act or process of creating ruches; fabric gathered to form folds
examples: The ruching on the bodice was carefully stitched.
They added ruching to the neckline.

senses.definition: plural of ruching; decorative gathered fabric elements
examples: The dress featured delicate ruchings along the seams.
They added several ruchings for texture.

senses.definition: a pile or stack; a crowd or mass of people; a reported danger zone; a fold or wrinkle in fabric
examples: A ruck of papers lay on the desk.
The hikers moved through a ruck of brush.
The fabric had a deep ruck near the seam.

senses.definition: to form a ruck; to crowd or push into a dense mass
examples: Fans ruck around the stage trying to get a better view.
The crowd rucked toward the exit.

senses.definition: having rucks or folds; creased or gathered fabric
examples: A rucked shirt looked casual and lived-in.
The skirt was neatly rucked at the side.
